timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Karen brings Eve Harrington, an admiring fan, to meet Margo Channing backstage, overcoming Eve's hesitation about meeting her idol.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Fan who speaks to Karen Richards outside the theater.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Karen decides to introduce Eve to Margo Channing and takes her backstage.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Entering the stage door to meet Margo Channing.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: How the introduction to Margo will proceed.
evidence_quotes: KAREN I'm going to take you to Margo...
Karen opens the door. They go in.

relation_updates: Establishes connection with Karen Richards
Secures introduction to Margo Channing
status_updates: From waiting fan to backstage invitee
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Devoted fan who has been watching the play and gains access to her idol.

timeline_nodes.scene_title: 6、INT. MARGO'S DRESSING ROOM - CURRAN THEATER - NIGHT
timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Karen and Lloyd bid Eve emotional farewells, expressing mutual admiration; Eve thanks Karen for making the night unforgettable, and they part with warm wishes for future friendship.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Fan introduced to Margo Channing and associates in dressing room
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Eve is formally introduced to the group; Margo adopts a theatrical, gracious manner, while Birdie expresses cynicism.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Eve is seated and offered a drink; the group is engaged but slightly flustered, with Eve remaining calm.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: How Eve will further interact with Margo and the group is unclear.
evidence_quotes: EVE (simply) I thought you'd forgotten about me.
KAREN Not at all. (her arm through Eve's) Margo, this is Eve Harrington.
MARGO (musically) How do you do, my dear.
MARGO Would you like a drink? It's right beside you...

relation_updates: Eve's story evokes sympathy from Margo, Lloyd, and Karen
Birdie remains cynical but is temporarily silenced
status_updates: Eve is perceived as a sympathetic figure with a tragic past
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Composed and attentive fan in a tense social introduction

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Bill departs as Eve and Margo leave; Eve comforts Margo, who later moves Eve's belongings into her guest room.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Eve comforts Margo after Bill's departure and moves into her home as a live-in companion.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Bill entrusts Eve to watch over Margo, and Eve moves into Margo's guest room.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Eve is living in Margo's guest room and has been tasked by Bill to keep Margo company.
evidence_quotes: BILL Keep your eyes on her. Don't let her get lonely. She's a loose lamb in a jungle...
EVE Don't worry...

relation_updates: Bill entrusts Eve with watching over Margo
Eve comforts Margo after Bill's departure
status_updates: Eve moves into Margo's guest room
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Live-in companion entrusted by Bill.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Margo catches Eve mimicking her in her dress, reacts with understanding, and quietly suggests Mrs. Brown handle the wardrobe, prompting Eve to silently comply.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Aspiring actress caught in private imitation of Margo
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Margo discovers Eve privately mimicking her stage performance with the dress and suggests Mrs. Brown handle the wardrobe.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Eve silently complies by returning the dress to Margo.
evidence_quotes: She has Margo's dress held up against her body.
She turns this way and that, bows as if to applause - mimicking Margo exactly...
MARGO watches her curiously.
EVE, startled, whips the gown away, turns to Margo.

relation_updates: Margo witnesses Eve's private fantasy of being her
status_updates: Caught in an act of revealing personal aspiration
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Secretly ambitious imitator

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Karen and Birdie discuss actors' hardships, contrasting Hollywood with New York, as they exit down the stairs and out of view.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Assistant to Margo Channing seeking to become her understudy
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Eve directly asks Karen to advocate for her as Margo's understudy, citing her familiarity with the role and staging.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Karen has agreed to speak to Mr. Fabian about Eve becoming Margo's understudy.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Whether Eve will secure the understudy position
evidence_quotes: isn't it awful, I'm about to ask you for another favor - after all you've already done.
EVE Then - would you talk to Mr. Fabian about it? KAREN Of course.

relation_updates: Karen agrees to speak to Mr. Fabian about understudy role
Eve solidifies alliance with Karen through favor request
status_updates: Seeks transition from personal assistant to stage understudy
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Aspiring actress actively pursuing stage opportunity

timeline_nodes.scene_title: 29、INT. LOBBY AND FOYER - CURRAN THEATER - DAY
timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Margo leaves abruptly; Addison escorts Miss Caswell, who inquires about TV auditions, and he confirms they are constant, leading her toward the street.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Margo Channing's understudy who read in Miss Caswell's audition
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Addison reveals to Margo that Eve is her new understudy and that Eve participated in the audition, surprising Margo who feigns prior knowledge.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Eve is officially Margo's understudy, but Margo's reaction is concealed.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Margo's genuine reaction to Eve's understudy role and audition participation.
evidence_quotes: MARGO Eve! My understudy... ADDISON (keenly) Didn't you know? MARGO (quickly) Of course I knew. ADDISON It just slipped your mind.

relation_updates: Margo feigns prior knowledge of Eve's understudy role after initial surprise
status_updates: Eve is now officially Margo Channing's understudy
Eve participated in a professional audition
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Understudy for Margo Channing with audition experience.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Eve blackmails Karen into supporting her for the role of Cora by threatening to expose Karen's and Addison's sabotage of Margo, revealing her willingness to go to extreme lengths for the part.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Blackmailer coercing Karen to support her casting as Cora
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Eve blackmails Karen by threatening to expose Karen's sabotage of Margo, forcing Karen to agree to advocate for Eve's role as Cora.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To secure the role of Cora
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Karen has been coerced into agreeing to support Eve's casting
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Whether Karen will follow through and if Margo discovers the truth
evidence_quotes: I'd do much more - for a part that good.
Eve has reached up to take Karen's hand. She holds it now, as she turns slowly to face her.

relation_updates: Adversarial relationship with Karen established via blackmail
Cooperative alliance with Addison leveraged as threat
status_updates: Now actively manipulating others through coercion
Secured Karen's reluctant support for the role
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Manipulator who uses blackmail to achieve her goals

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Karen reflects on how Eve's involvement caused tension between Bill and Lloyd, with Bill initially refusing to direct, Lloyd meddling, and rehearsals strained, yet Eve kept the production going despite growing conflict.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Lead actress who intervenes in director-playwright conflicts
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Triggers a blocking dispute by moving upstage, then reconciles Bill and Lloyd by fetching Bill and whispering to Lloyd
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To perform her role and resolve the immediate conflict
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Rehearsals resume with Bill directing and Lloyd appeased
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Ongoing tensions between Bill and Lloyd over Eve's role
evidence_quotes: As she speaks, Eve moves upstage, turns to face the young man who is forced to turn his back to the auditorium.
Eve seems to be at a loss. She looks at Lloyd.
Eve drags Bill back.
Eve whispers to Lloyd from the stage.

relation_updates: Working relationship with Bill where she can influence him
Cooperative bond with Lloyd who supports her creative choices
status_updates: Now the lead actress in the production
Actively managing interpersonal conflicts to protect her role
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Lead actress and conflict mediator

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Addison asserts dominance over Eve, acknowledging their shared flaws and ambition, while Eve, emotionally broken, doubts her ability to perform, but Addison insists she will deliver the performance of her life.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Eve submits to Addison after he exposes her lies and she breaks down crying.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Addison confronts Eve with her lies and manipulations, leading her to emotionally collapse and submit, acknowledging she belongs to him.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Eve is lying on the bed, tear-stained and listless, having submitted to Addison, but doubting her ability to perform that night.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Whether Eve can emotionally recover to perform as Addison insists.
evidence_quotes: Eve throws herself on the bed, face down, bursts in tears.
EVE Yes, Addison.
ADDISON And you realize - you agree how completely you belong to me? EVE Yes, Addison.
EVE (tonelessly) I won't play tonight.

relation_updates: Eve submits to Addison's control
Addison establishes complete dominance over Eve
status_updates: Eve is emotionally broken
Eve acknowledges belonging to Addison
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Eve, with tear-stained face, submits tonelessly to Addison's control.

timeline_nodes.scene_title: 51、INT. DINING HALL - SARAH SIDDONS SOCIETY - NIGHT
timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Eve, emotionally detached after the ceremony, declines to attend Max's party despite Addison's urging, hands him her award, and leaves, expressing fatigue and desire to go home.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Award winner giving acceptance speech
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Eve accepts the Sarah Siddons Award and delivers a speech thanking colleagues, while announcing her upcoming film work in Hollywood.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Eve is at the ceremony posing for pictures with the award after her speech.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Whether her expressed commitment to the Theater is genuine or a facade.
evidence_quotes: Eve accepts the award from the Aged Actor, kisses him tenderly, folds the award to her bosom and waits for quiet.
She speaks with assurance, yet modestly and humbly.

relation_updates: Margo's sarcastic remark reinforces adversarial stance
Addison's urging to attend party is refused, straining their relationship
status_updates: Eve gives away the Sarah Siddons Award
Eve departs the banquet venue
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Award-winning actress who balances public humility with private ambition.

focal_character_timelines.timeline_summary: Eve Harrington begins as an obsessive fan who infiltrates Margo Channing's inner circle, becoming her assistant and later understudy. Through manipulation, blackmail, and strategic alliances—particularly with critic Addison deWitt—she rises to star in Lloyd Richards' play and wins the prestigious Sarah Siddons Award. Her ascent leaves her emotionally detached and ultimately under Addison's control, mirroring the cycle of ambition she once admired.

focal_character_timelines.selection_reason: Aligned with the movie's ICRP character set.
focal_character_timelines.task3_relevance: Directly aligned with the same key character used in ICRP.

timeline_nodes.scene_title: 51、INT. DINING HALL - SARAH SIDDONS SOCIETY - NIGHT
timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Eve, emotionally detached after the ceremony, declines to attend Max's party despite Addison's urging, hands him her award, and leaves, expressing fatigue and desire to go home.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Lloyd's wife, publicly reconciled at the ceremony
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Lloyd kisses Karen and gives her the award; Karen congratulates Eve before leaving with Lloyd.

He gives her the award.
Karen pauses at Eve. KAREN Congratulations, Eve.

Maintains polite public facade with Eve

Publicly associated with Eve's success despite private knowledge

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Karen brings Eve Harrington, an admiring fan, to meet Margo backstage, despite Eve's hesitation about intruding and her appearance.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Karen acts as a benefactor by introducing Eve to Margo's circle.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Karen meets Eve Harrington outside the theater, learns she is a devoted fan who sees the play regularly, and insists on bringing her backstage to meet Margo.

EVE'S VOICE (softly) Mrs. Richards...
Karen practically propels her toward the stage door.
Karen opens the door. They go in.

relation_updates: Initiates relationship with Eve

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Eve nearly collides with Birdie, who mocks a fur coat's extravagance; Karen notes actors' infrequent meals, Birdie jokes about oranges, and they exit down the stairs.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Advocate for Eve's career
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Karen agrees to speak to producer Max Fabian about making Eve Margo's understudy when Eve asks her directly.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To get Eve appointed as Margo's understudy
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Karen has agreed to talk to Fabian on Eve's behalf

Assumes responsibility for advancing Eve's career
status_updates: Becomes active agent in Eve's advancement
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Friend who uses her connections to assist Eve

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Karen dials a number from a leather phone book, politely requesting to speak with Eve Harrington, her call deliberate and purposeful as she waits.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Contemplating a corrective plan for Margo
timeline_nodes.salient_development: After listening to Lloyd's frustrations about Margo, Karen thinks about using Eve to give Margo 'the boot in the rear' she believes Margo deserves.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To give Margo 'the boot in the rear she needs and deserves'
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Karen has an idea to use Eve against Margo but is still considering whether to proceed.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Whether Karen will act on her idea and what might happen if she does.
evidence_quotes: KAREN'S VOICE That boot in the rear to Margo. Heaven knows she had one coming.

relation_updates: Shifts from loyal friend to active manipulator regarding Margo
status_updates: Transitions from passive observer to active plotter
auxiliary.persona_anchor: A loyal friend now weighing a calculated move

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Lloyd reassures Karen he won't replace her with Eve, citing her approval as key, which restores Karen's confidence and strengthens their relationship.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Wife asserting control over casting decisions
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Lloyd reassures Karen that he will not cast Eve without her approval, confirming Karen's authority over the role.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To ensure Margo is cast and prevent Eve from getting the role
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Karen is pleased and holds gatekeeping authority over Eve's future requests for the role
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Eve may still seek the role, but Karen now controls access
evidence_quotes: KAREN That's fine. Fine and dandy. I'd enjoy nothing more. Just refer all of Miss Harrington's future requests to me...

relation_updates: Conflict with Lloyd resolved with her authority affirmed
Adversarial stance toward Eve solidified
status_updates: Gains gatekeeping authority over Eve's requests
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Protective wife and collaborator in casting

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Eve blackmails Karen into supporting her for the role of Cora by threatening to expose Karen's and Addison's sabotage of Margo, offering silence in exchange for the part.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Blackmailed accomplice
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Eve blackmails Karen by threatening to expose Karen's and Addison's sabotage of Margo, forcing Karen to agree to recommend Eve for the role of Cora.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To protect her secret by complying with Eve's demand to recommend Eve for the role.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Karen is compelled to recommend Eve for the part, betraying Margo.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: How to manage the betrayal and its impact on her friendship with Margo.
evidence_quotes: KAREN A part in a play. You'd do all that - just for a part in a play.

relation_updates: Eve becomes a coercive blackmailer
Friendship with Margo is now at risk due to forced betrayal
status_updates: Now complicit in Eve's scheme
Carries a damaging secret
auxiliary.persona_anchor: A compromised friend forced into betrayal.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Margo declines the role of Cora, citing her new married life, and seeks Lloyd's understanding; Karen reacts with hysterical laughter, leaving tension unresolved as the scene ends.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Present when Margo declines the role of Cora
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Karen reacts with shock and hysterical laughter to Margo's announcement that she will not play Cora
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Karen is laughing hysterically as Margo removes her champagne glass, with tension unresolved
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: The reason for Karen's hysterical laughter is not addressed
evidence_quotes: KAREN (explodes) What?
bursts into hysterical laughter...

relation_updates: Reminded of her betrayal of Margo

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Lloyd leaves to be with Eve despite Karen's plea, confirming her fears that his loyalty lies with Eve over their marriage, deepening the rift between them.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Wife pleading with husband not to leave for another woman
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Karen pleads with Lloyd not to go to Eve in the middle of the night, but he dismisses her concerns and leaves.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To prevent Lloyd from leaving for Eve
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Lloyd departs for Eve; Karen is left despairing

Lloyd goes out. Karen looks after him, despairing.

relation_updates: Lloyd chooses Eve over Karen
Marriage reaches crisis point
status_updates: Left despairing after Lloyd's departure
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Desperate wife in marital crisis

focal_character_timelines.timeline_summary: Karen Richards begins as Eve Harrington's champion, introducing her to the theater world and advocating for her career. Her loyalty shifts to conflict as she witnesses Eve's manipulations and is later blackmailed into supporting Eve's ambitions, straining her marriage to Lloyd and her friendship with Margo. Ultimately, Karen is publicly honored by Eve while privately reconciled with Lloyd, carrying the weight of past betrayals.

focal_character_timelines.selection_reason: Aligned with the movie's ICRP character set.
focal_character_timelines.task3_relevance: Directly aligned with the same key character used in ICRP.

timeline_nodes.scene_title: 6、INT. MARGO'S DRESSING ROOM - CURRAN THEATER - NIGHT
timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Karen and Lloyd bid Eve emotional farewells, expressing mutual admiration; Eve thanks Karen for making the night unforgettable, and they part with warm wishes for future friendship.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Margo Channing meets fan Eve Harrington in her dressing room after a performance.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Margo initially reacts with skepticism to Eve's presence but switches to a polite, performative welcome when Eve enters.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Margo has met Eve and is engaging with her in conversation.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: How will Margo's relationship with Eve develop from this meeting?
evidence_quotes: MARGO (to Birdie; also a whisper) The heave-ho.
MARGO (musically) How do you do, my dear.
MARGO Would you like a drink? It's right beside you...

relation_updates: Margo meets Eve Harrington, becomes protective of her, and invites her into her life
status_updates: Margo is in her dressing room after a performance
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Margo's tendency to switch between candid and performative personas depending on the audience.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Margo and Bill bicker playfully about jealousy and aging, but their moment is interrupted by Eve's arrival with Bill's ticket, prompting them to head toward the gate.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Margo expresses jealousy and insecurity as Bill prepares to leave.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Margo tells Bill she has developed a protective feeling for Eve and worries he might be attracted to younger women.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: Seeking reassurance from Bill about their relationship.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Margo is interrupted by Eve's arrival and heads to the gate with Bill, her anxieties unresolved.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Whether Bill will remain faithful during his trip.
evidence_quotes: MARGO Don't get stuck on some glamour puss-
MARGO Am I going to lose you, Bill? Am I?
isn't it silly, suddenly I've developed a big protective feeling for her - a lamb loose in our big stone jungle...

relation_updates: Margo reveals her insecurities to Bill and her growing attachment to Eve
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Margo's fear of aging and being replaced by younger women.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Bill departs as Eve and Margo leave; Eve comforts Margo, who later moves Eve's belongings into her guest room.

timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Eve is now living in Margo's guest room after Bill's departure.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Margo's potential loneliness after Bill leaves, as indicated by Bill's instruction to Eve.

They kiss.
Margo's arms tighten desperately.
That same night we sent for Eve's things, her few pitiful possessions... she moved into the little guest room on the top floor...

Eve becomes Margo's live-in companion
status_updates: Eve moves into Margo's guest room
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Margo's acceptance of Eve's companionship, evidenced by her smile and arranging for Eve to move in.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Margo awkwardly wishes Bill happy birthday after he reveals Eve has been writing to him weekly and organizing his welcome-home party; they exchange strained goodbyes.

timeline_nodes.salient_development: Margo discovers that Eve has been writing to Bill weekly and organizing his welcome-home party, making Margo feel excluded

Eve revealed as primary correspondent with Bill
status_updates: Becomes aware of Eve's independent contact with Bill

timeline_nodes.scene_title: 20、INT. SECOND FLOOR LANDING AND STAIRS - NIGHT

timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Intoxicated host confronts guests with sarcastic remarks
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Margo's drunken state sparks arguments with Bill and Karen, and she sarcastically provokes Eve, escalating tensions at the party

Karen directly challenges Margo
Margo sarcastically provokes Eve

Eve seated, looking up fascinated at Bill as he talks

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Bill leaves Margo after a tense confrontation, citing disbelief in their unresolved conflict; Margo calls after him, but he departs, leaving her in tears.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Lead actress discovering during rehearsal that Eve is her understudy
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Margo learns from Lloyd that Eve has been appointed her understudy without her knowledge, reacts with surprise and sarcasm, escalating tension with Lloyd and Bill.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Margo is on stage, aware of Eve's understudy role, with relationships with Lloyd and Bill strained.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: The conflict with Lloyd and Bill over Eve's appointment and Margo's professional standing.

MARGO What's all over?

relation_updates: Margo's relationship with Lloyd becomes hostile
Margo's relationship with Bill reaches breaking point
Eve is now officially Margo's understudy
status_updates: Margo is on stage, aware of the deception, with growing suspicion toward Eve and resentment toward colleagues
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Margo's pride and fear of replacement manifest in defensive sarcasm and confrontation.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Margo dismisses Karen's apology for their stranded situation, attributing it to fate, while both settle in uncomfortably, hinting at underlying tension.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Margo confides in Karen about her insecurities regarding her identity and relationship with Bill.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Margo apologizes for her recent behavior, expresses confusion about her true self, and fears that Bill loves her public persona, which she believes will fade with age.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: Seeking reassurance and understanding from Karen about her fears.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Vulnerable and uncertain, as indicated by her turning on the radio and smoking after the conversation.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Whether Bill can love her beyond her star persona as she ages.
evidence_quotes: I haven't been pleasant this weekend.
So many people - know me. I wish I did. I wish someone would tell be about me...
Those years stretch as the years go on. I've seen it happen too often.

relation_updates: Apologizes to Karen for recent unpleasant behavior
Expresses fear that Bill loves 'Margo Channing' the star, not her true self
Acknowledges treating Eve badly
status_updates: Stranded in car with Karen and Lloyd
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Margo's fear of losing her identity and being loved only for her public image.

timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Margo decides to leave her stage role to embrace married life, causing emotional tension; Karen reacts with hysterical laughter, leaving Lloyd unable to respond.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Margo declares she will not play the role of Cora, choosing to become a married woman instead.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Margo states she will not play Cora, emphasizing her desire to be a 'married lady' and leave stage roles she feels too old for.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: To have a home and a life with Bill, and stop pretending on and off stage.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Margo's decision is met with Karen's hysterical laughter, leaving Lloyd unable to respond and the situation unresolved.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Lloyd's reaction to Margo's withdrawal from the role is interrupted by Karen's outburst.
evidence_quotes: MARGO A married lady.
MARGO I'm going to have a home. Not just a house I'm afraid to stay in... and a man to go with it.
MARGO Well. I don't want to play \"Cora.\"

relation_updates: Seeks Lloyd's forgiveness for abandoning Cora role
Affirms commitment to Bill as husband
status_updates: Retires from playing youthful stage roles
Transitions from actress to prospective married woman
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Margo prioritizes personal fulfillment and authenticity over professional acting commitments.

timeline_nodes.scene_title: 51、INT. DINING HALL - SARAH SIDDONS SOCIETY - NIGHT
timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Eve, emotionally detached after the ceremony, declines to attend Max's party despite Addison's urging, hands him her award, and leaves, expressing fatigue and desire to go home.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: Theater star interacting with Eve after the award ceremony
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Margo makes a sarcastic remark to Eve about her speech and award, suggesting Eve lacks heart.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Margo leaves with Bill after the remark, while Eve remains wordless.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Margo's resentment toward Eve for her success and perceived insincerity.
evidence_quotes: MARGO ... nice speech, Eve. But I wouldn't worry too much about your heart. You can always put that award where your heart ought to be.
Eve looks at her wordlessly.
Margo and Bill leave.

relation_updates: Eve publicly acknowledges Margo as her idol and benefactor
Margo's grim smile and cutting remark show unresolved tension
status_updates: Present at ceremony where Eve receives award for role originally intended for Margo
auxiliary.persona_anchor: Margo's pride and bitterness evident in her cutting comment.

focal_character_timelines.timeline_summary: Margo Channing, an established theater star, takes the young Eve Harrington under her wing, but her initial empathy turns to suspicion and jealousy as Eve becomes involved in her personal and professional life. Margo's insecurities about aging and her relationship with Bill lead to a series of confrontations, culminating in a professional crisis when Eve becomes her understudy. After a period of self-reflection, Margo chooses marriage over her career, but her resentment toward Eve persists, as seen in her cutting remark at the award ceremony.
